name: Brownie Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the chocolate in a double boiler over hot water. 2. Sift together the flour, baking soda, and baking powder. Set aside. 3. Cream the butter until smooth. Gradually add sugar and continue beating. Add vanilla extract, then mix in eggs one at a time. Mix in the melted chocolate until fully blended. 4. Fold in the dry ingredients mixture by hand. Stir in milk until just combined. 5. Gently fold in chopped walnuts. Cover and refrigerate dough for 30 minutes before rolling out. 6. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease a baking sheet lightly. 7. Roll out chilled dough on a well-floured surface until it's about 1/4 inch thick. Cut into desired shapes or rounds, then re-roll any scraps. 8. Transfer cookies to the prepared sheet, leaving space for expansion. Bake for 6 to 8 minutes, checking for any hot spots to prevent overbrowning. 9. Variation: For a brownie cookie crust, chill dough briefly. Roll out on a baking sheet, bake until firm (5-7 minutes). Let cool completely and crush into fine crumbs (about 2 1/4 cups). Melt butter and mix with crumbs. Pour mixture into a springform pan base and sides. Freeze for 30 minutes before filling.
Note: Individual cookie crumb crusts can be made using a muffin pan lined with wax paper for the "Kit's Chocolate Mousse" recipe.
